title: Magnetic interactions and spin orders in Cr$_8$ and V$_8$ ring-shaped molecular magnets from non-collinear ab initio calculations
authors: Maria Barbara Maccioni, Elia Stocco, Luca Binci, Andrea Floris, Matteo Cococcioni
abstract: We employ density functional theory within a non-collinear framework to investigate the magnetic properties of the octanuclear molecular rings Cr$_8$ and V$_8$. Our aim is to generalize the evaluation of the effective magnetic interactions by explicitly including non-collinear spin configurations, thereby refining our understanding of their dependence upon the underlying electronic structure and molecular geometry. By analyzing the energetics of a variety of magnetic configurations, particularly non-collinear arrangements with neighboring spins oriented along different directions, we move beyond the exchange-only Heisenberg Hamiltonian describing the low-energy sector of the excitation spectrum. This approach enables us to distinguish between in-plane and out-of-plane exchange interactions, and to incorporate biquadratic coupling terms into the effective spin Hamiltonian. We reveal significant antisymmetric exchange interactions of the Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya (DM) type whose dependence on the curvature of the annular structure is clarified by a comparison with the results obtained from linear chains of equal composition. Our work demonstrates that interactions beyond conventional exchange, particularly biquadratic anisotropic terms, in the spin Hamiltonian are essential for accurately capturing the low-energy excitations of these systems. The closest quantitative agreement with experimental results (particularly for the case of Cr$_8$) is achieved when extended Hubbard functionals are used for the evaluation of the effective magnetic couplings.
summary: This paper investigates the magnetic properties of Cr8 and V8 molecular rings using advanced non-collinear DFT calculations. It moves beyond the simple Heisenberg model to include biquadratic and Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interactions. The study reveals that while Cr8's magnetic anisotropy is largely geometric, V8 possesses intrinsic, complex interactions that significantly alter its ground state. The use of extended Hubbard functionals (LDA+U+V) is shown to be crucial for accurate quantitative agreement with experimental data.
main_problem: The study aims to refine the magnetic description of Cr8 and V8 molecular rings by investigating interactions beyond the standard Heisenberg exchange, specifically biquadratic coupling and Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya (DM) interactions.
main_result: The researchers found that biquadratic terms are essential for capturing low-energy excitations in V8, and that DM interactions in Cr8 have a geometric origin due to structural curvature, whereas in V8 they have an intrinsic electronic origin.
method: Non-collinear Density Functional Theory (DFT) using LDA+U and LDA+U+V functionals, combined with spin rotation methods and exact diagonalization of an extended spin Hamiltonian.
model_or_system: Octanuclear ring-shaped molecular magnets Cr8 (antiferromagnetic) and V8 (ferromagnetic/antiferromagnetic transition), modeled using an extended spin Hamiltonian including Heisenberg, biquadratic, and DM terms.
key_observables: Magnetic susceptibility (chi and chiT), total magnetization, energy spectra, and exchange coupling constants (J1 through J4).
important_parameters: Exchange couplings (J), biquadratic parameters (BQ), DM interaction components (dr, dt, dz), and Hubbard parameters (U, V).
main_assumptions: Use of hydrogen saturation to simplify organic ligands and the use of the Local Density Approximation (LDA) for exchange-correlation energy.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the polyhedral structure of the M8 rings; Figure 2 shows the linear chain models; Figure 7 illustrates spin configurations for DM extraction; Figure 13 compares magnetic susceptibility across different Hamiltonian approximations; Figure 14 shows spin-resolved energy spectra.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the molecular systems, describes the non-collinear DFT methodology, analyzes the magnetic interactions and energy profiles of Cr8, investigates the complex biquadratic and DM interactions in V8, and concludes with a comparison of different Hamiltonian models and their agreement with experimental susceptibility.
why_interesting: The paper provides deep insights into how structural geometry and electronic correlations (via Hubbard U and V) generate complex, non-collinear magnetic interactions, which is highly relevant to understanding many-body dynamics and spin-spin coupling in molecular-scale systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

FIG. 4. Non-collinear magnetic setups, in V8 radial configu- ration, considered to calculate the exchange parameters be- tween all neighbor magnetic sites, J2, J3 and J4 .

FIG. 5. Non-collinear DFT total energy profiles obtained rotating a single spin in Cr8 starting from the AFM ground state configuration in each direction. Dots are results from ab initio calculations; dashed curves are fit obtained from Eq. (7).

FIG. 6. Non-collinear DFT total energy profiles obtained rotating a single spin in V8 starting from the FM ground state configuration in each direction. Dots are results from ab initio calculations; dashed curves are fit obtained from Eq. (7).
FIG. 8. Helical and non-helical spin configurations used to to evaluate the dz antisymmetric exchange couplings in chain model.
FIG. 7. Magnetic spin configurations used to evaluate dz in Cr8 and V8

FIG. 9. Magnetic susceptibility of Cr8. The plots shows the magnetic susceptibility χ weighted by the temperature T as obtained from LDA+U (left) and LDA+U+V (right) by considering progressively only some of the computed interactions. The solid purple line correspond to a system of free spins and therefore the susceptibility recovers the Curie law. The curve ex(1) correspond to an anisotropic Heisenberg hamiltonian with only the 11 nearest neighbor interactions whose parameters are reported in Table II. The curve ex(1-4) includes also the 2nd, 3rd and 4th nearest neighbor interactions reported in Tables III and IV. Finally, ex(1-4)+Bq includes biquadratic and ex(1-4)+Bq+DM also DM...
FIG. 10. Magnetic susceptibility of V8. The same legend and considerations of Figure 9 also apply here.

FIG. 12. Comparison of fitted energy curves with and without off-diagonal exchange terms (tangential configuration) for the V8 system. A single spin is being rotated from its reference orientation. Data points are the same shown in Fig. 6 of the main text.
